title: Cherry Bakewell tart
subtitle: Frangipane on raspberry jam in a shortcrust shell
excerpt: A classic British Bakewell tart: a shortcrust pastry shell, a thin layer of raspberry jam, an almond frangipane filling, and glacé cherries pressed on top. One 23 cm tart, serving eight.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: documents the Derbyshire Bakewell pudding and its later tart variant. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932): notes the cherry-Bakewell form as a household adaptation. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845): the almond-frangipane filling on jam.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the baked tart whole, wrapped twice; defrost in the fridge.

glossaryTerms.definition: An almond cream made from creamed butter and sugar beaten with eggs, ground almonds, and a little flour. Spread into a pastry shell over jam or fresh fruit and baked, it sets to a moist, soft, dense almond filling — closer to a baked cake batter than a custard. The classic French and British pastry filling for almond tarts.

content.text: A cherry Bakewell is the popularised tart form of the Derbyshire Bakewell pudding: shortcrust base, raspberry jam, almond frangipane, glacé cherries on top. Where the pudding has a custard filling on jam, the tart swaps in frangipane — a moist almond cake batter that bakes soft and dense.

Iced Bakewell adds a thin layer of white glacé icing over the cooled frangipane with a single glacé cherry pressed in the centre. The version here keeps the cherries inside the bake; both are correct.

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}}, {{caster-sugar}} and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Bind with the yolk of the {{eggs}} and {{water}}. Chill 30 minutes. Heat oven to 180°C fan. Roll, line a 23 cm tart tin, trim and prick. Blind-bake with baking beans for 12 minutes, remove paper and beans and bake another 5 minutes.

Beat the softened second {{unsalted-butter}} with the second {{caster-sugar}} until creamed. Beat in the two {{eggs}} one at a time. Fold in the {{almond-flour}}, second {{plain-flour}} and {{almond-extract}} — this is the
. Spread the {{raspberry-jam}} over the warm pastry base. Spoon the frangipane over the jam and level. Press the halved {{glace-cherries}} cut-side-up across the top. Scatter the {{almonds-flaked}}.
Bake for 25–30 minutes; the frangipane should be deep gold, set in the centre, and a skewer should come out clean. Cool fully in the tin before slicing.
